footer.tagline: App Router-native MDX documentation for Next.js

marketingHome.heroDescription: EmcyDocs is an App Router-native MDX documentation library for Next.js. File-based content, locale-aware routes, a flexible theme system, and a mobile-first UX all in one package.
marketingHome.layoutsDescription: Shape the experience with theme presets, density, shell sizing, and optional custom header or sidebar overrides.

layoutCards.title: Classic
layoutCards.description: The core docs experience with sidebar, search, TOC, and shared mobile UX.

layoutCards.title: Theme system
layoutCards.description: Preset, mode, density, radius, accent hue, and shell sizing from one theme object.

layoutCards.title: Custom chrome
layoutCards.description: Replace the default header or sidebar with your own component or leave them blank.

layoutCards.title: Locale routing
layoutCards.description: Keep the default locale clean while `es` and `zh` live on explicit route prefixes.

marketingHome.featuresDescription: Everything you need to build great documentation, without the boilerplate.

features.title: App Router Native
features.description: Generate metadata and static params from the same content source. First-class RSC support.

features.title: File-based Locales
features.description: Keep en.mdx and es.mdx in the same folder. Routes handle locale prefixes automatically.

features.title: Mobile-first UX
features.description: Built-in mobile nav, search, sticky chrome, and scroll locking. No rebuilding required.

features.title: Section Deep Links
features.description: Search results jump directly to matching section anchors with useful snippets.

features.title: Progressive Disclosure
features.description: Use createDocsSource for the common path, override when you need more control.

features.title: Battle Tested
features.description: Dogfooded against real apps, so the API handles route quirks, custom chrome, and real content.

marketingHome.ctaDescription: Get started in minutes. Install the package, create your first MDX file, and you are live.

docsClassicHome.title: Localized docs with route-aware locale prefixes
docsClassicHome.description: The default locale stays clean at /docs while non-default locales can live under prefixed routes without changing the content model.
